lede: A look at June 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Barbara (singer) and Peter the Great, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Nadahan wedding bombing
events.desc: At least 40 people are killed and more than 70 wounded in a suicide bombing at a wedding party in Arghandab, Kandahar.

events.title: Secretariat (horse)
events.desc: In horse racing, Secretariat wins the U.S. Triple Crown.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Ninety-nine civilians are hanged from lampposts and balconies by German troops in Tulle, France, in reprisal for maquisards attacks.

events.title: Charles Kingsford Smith
events.desc: Charles Kingsford Smith completes the first trans-Pacific flight in a Fokker Trimotor monoplane, the Southern Cross.

events.title: Bulgaria
events.desc: Bulgaria's military takes over the government in a coup.

events.title: Åland
events.desc: Åland's Regional Assembly convened for its first plenary session in Mariehamn, Åland; today, the day is celebrated as Self-Government Day of Åland.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Stonewall Jackson concludes his successful Shenandoah Valley Campaign with a victory in the Battle of Port Republic.

events.title: Mormon handcart pioneers
events.desc: Five hundred Mormons leave Iowa City, Iowa for the Mormon Trail.

events.title: Simon de Colines
events.desc: The Parisian Faculty of Theology fines Simon de Colines for publishing the Biblical commentary Commentarii initiatorii in quatuor Evangelia by Jacques Lefèvre d'Étaples.

events.title: AD 747
events.desc: Abbasid Revolution: Abu Muslim Khorasani begins an open revolt against Umayyad rule, which is carried out under the sign of the Black Standard.

births.name: Bertha von Suttner
births.line: Austrian journalist and author, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Walter Jens, German philologist, historian, and academic
Miguel Ángel Asturias, Guatemalan journalist, author, and poet, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Anniversary of the Accession of King Abdullah II (Jordan)
Christian feast day: Aidan of Lindisfarne (Evangelical Lutheran Church in America)
Christian feast day: Blessed Anna Maria Taigi
Christian feast day: Bede (Evangelical Lutheran Church in America)
Christian feast day: Ephrem the Syrian (Roman Catholic Church and Church of England)
